title: dysoxylum cyrtobotryum
intro: Dysoxylum cyrtobotryum, also known as the Indian mastich tree, is a deciduous tree native to the tropical forests of Asia, particularly in India and Southeast Asia. The plant's bark and leaves have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for their medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Dysoxylum cyrtobotryum include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve stress. Dysoxylum cyrtobotryum can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
